channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Australia's prime minister is criticized for buying a waterfront home during a housing crisis
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has been criticized for buying a multimillion-dollar waterfront home during a national housing crisis with federal elections just months away.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: First Italian ship with 16 intercepted migrants docks at Albanian port to process asylum request
item.description: An Italian navy ship docked at the Albanian port of Shengjin to bring the first group of 16 migrants intercepted in international waters.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Zelenskyy to unveil 'victory plan' to Ukrainian lawmakers after presenting it to Western allies
item.description: The plan is considered by many as Ukraine’s last resort to strengthen its hand in any future cease-fire negotiations.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: UN says Yemen risks being dragged into Mideast conflict that could spiral out of control
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — Yemen risks being dragged further into the military escalation in the Middle East that keeps intensifying and could spiral out of control, the U.N. special envoy for the Arab world’s poorest nation said Tuesday. Hans Grundberg told the U.N.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexican president acknowledges army killings of 3 civilians in a violent border city
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexico’s new president acknowledged Tuesday that three civilians including a child died in two shootouts involving the military last week in a violent border city.
item.dc:creator: FABIOLA SÁNCHEZ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Canada-India ties could take a long time to recover
item.description: India and Canada have expelled each other's top diplomats over the 2023 assassination of Sikh activist Hardeep Singh Nijjar and allegations of other crimes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea blows up parts of inter-Korean road and rail links in a symbolic display of anger
item.description: In a move that underlines North Korea's growing anger with South Korea, North Korea has blown up sections of unused rail routes and roads that once connected the two countries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia releases man whose daughter's drawing opposed Ukraine fighting
item.description: A group that monitors political detentions in Russia says that a man convicted of discrediting the military after his daughter made a drawing criticizing Russia’s military actions in Ukraine has been released from prison after serving 22 months.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Giant pandas arrive at National Zoo
item.description: Two giant pandas arrived at the National Zoo in Washington, D.C. on Tuesday after a flight from China, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Iran terror proxies amass on Israel’s borders in 'Ring of Fire’
item.description: Iran has developed the so called "Ring of Fire" around Israel to further expand its influence throughout the region. Experts note that violent terror groups sit across the Jewish state's borders.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hereditary nobles have sat in Britain's Parliament for centuries. Their time may be up
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Like his ancestors for centuries, the Earl of Devon serves in Parliament, helping to make the laws of the land. But not for much longer.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Committee investigating Indian assassination plot meeting officials in Washington
item.description: An Indian government committee investigating Indian involvement in a foiled murder plot against a prominent activist in New York City will meet U.S. officials in Washington this week, the State Department announced.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Kenya's High Court rejects move to stop deputy president's impeachment debate
item.description: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — Kenya's High Court on Tuesday rejected an application by the deputy president's lawyers to stop the senate from debating an impeachment motion against him after parliament voted to remove him from office last week.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A Hong Kong zoo seeks answers after 9 monkeys die in 2 days
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Hong Kong's oldest zoo is seeking answers in a monkey medical mystery after nine animals died in two days, including three members of a critically endangered species.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Greek official accuses EU of policy failure on migration as war and climate change fuel displacement
item.description: ATHENS, Greece (AP) — A Greek official on Tuesday criticized the outgoing European Commission for failing to forge a common policy to deport migrants, and she warned that war and climate change were increasing global displacement.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korea blows up parts of inter-Korean road and rail links in a symbolic display of anger
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — In a symbolic display of anger, North Korea on Tuesday blew up the northern sections of unused road and rail routes that once linked it with South Korea, with the rivals exchanging threats days after the North claimed that the South flew drones over its capital Pyongyang.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Conkers controversy: World tournament investigates claims of cheating with steel chestnut
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The World Conker Championships said Tuesday that it was investigating allegations of cheating after this year's men’s winner was found to have a steel chestnut in his pocket.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Deadly Israel shooting ruled a terror attack
item.description: A police officer was killed and four others were injured when a terrorist opened fire near a highway in Ashdod, Israel, on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Southern Africa is enduring its worst hunger crisis in decades due to El Niño, the UN says
item.description: The United Nations' food agency says months of drought in southern Africa triggered by the El Niño weather phenomenon have had a devastating impact on more than 27 million people and caused the region’s worst hunger crisis in decades.
item.dc:creator: Gerald Imray, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Taliban-run media stops showing images of living beings in some Afghan provinces
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Taliban run-media have stopped showing images of living beings in some Afghan provinces to comply with morality laws, an official confirmed Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel reveals Hezbollah special forces terrorist 'bunker' located under home with weapons, motorcycles
item.description: Israel's military has uncovered a bunker it says Hezbollah's special forces unit was using to plan a cross-border attack on Israeli citizens.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Springfield rhetoric reverberates in Florida's Haitian community
item.description: Republican attacks on Haitian migrants in Ohio are reverberating in Florida, where Democrats are hoping to mobilize the country's largest Haitian American electorate to close the gap in their uphill battle for statewide races.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, The Hill

item.title: A pastry brought to Mexico by British miners is still popular after 200 years
item.description: REAL DEL MONTE, Mexico (AP) — Isabel Arriaga Lozano carefully fills a small pastry with a savory mix of meat, potatoes and chili pepper. She is crafting a “paste” (pronounced PAH-stay), a beloved Mexican snack with a rich history.
item.dc:creator: INDIA GRANT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China deploys record 125 warplanes in large-scale military drill in warning to Taiwan
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China employed a record 125 aircraft, as well as its Liaoning aircraft carrier and ships, in large-scale military exercises surrounding Taiwan and its outlying islands Monday, simulating the sealing off of key ports in a move that underscores the tense situation in the Taiwan S
item.dc:creator: JOHNSON LAI and HUIZHONG WU, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Campaigning begins for Japan's parliamentary election
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Official campaigning for Japan's Oct. 27 parliamentary election began Tuesday with new Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba seeking a mandate for his policies and for reforms after the governing party’s political funds scandal.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli strike in northern Lebanon kills at least 21 people
item.description: AITO, Lebanon (AP) — An Israeli airstrike hit an apartment building in northern Lebanon on Monday, killing at least 21 people, according to the Lebanese Red Cross. The Israeli military did not immediately comment and the target was not clear.
item.dc:creator: YAHYA HABCHITI, KAREEM CHEHAYEB, and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canada expels India's top diplomat and alleges wider diplomatic involvement in crimes
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canada said it has identified India’s top diplomat in the country as a person of interest in the assassination of a Sikh activist there and expelled him and five other diplomats Monday, in an escalating dispute over the June 2023 killing and allegations of other crimes.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, AIJAZ HUSSAIN and SHEIKH SAALIQ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pandas en route to National Zoo after a year
item.description: Two new giant pandas are reportedly en route to the National Zoo in Washington, D.C. “Something *giant* is coming to Washington, D.C. via the @FedEx Panda Express!” the zoo said in a post on social media platform X on Monday. “The Zoo will be closed to the public tomorrow, Oct. 15.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Italy sends the first ship with migrants to Albania as rights groups warn of a dangerous precedent
item.description: ROME (AP) — Italy is transferring the first group of migrants to Albania, the Interior Ministry said Monday, as part of a contentious plan to process thousands of asylum-seekers outside its borders.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: French citizen convicted in Russia of collecting military information and gets 3 years in prison
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — A Russian court on Monday convicted a French citizen of collecting military information and sentenced him to three years in prison. Laurent Vinatier, who was arrested in Moscow in June, earlier admitted guilt, setting the stage for a fast-tracked trial.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia says it captured a southern Ukraine village in a push before winter comes
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia said Monday it captured the village of Levadne in southern Ukraine as it probes for weaknesses along the war ’s roughly 1,000-kilometer (600-mile) front line, including in eastern areas that are the main focus of Moscow’s military effort before winter arrives.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UNIFIL ignored Hezbollah terror build up for 18 years, Israel's UN ambassador says
item.description: Amid fighting between Israel and Hezbollah terrorists, the U.N. is facing criticism that its peacekeeping force has failed to stop the Iran-terror proxy's military build up in southern Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US raises concern over Chinese military drills near Taiwan
item.description: The United States said it is “seriously concerned” about Chinese military drills near Taiwan on Monday. State Department spokesperson Matthew Miller said the U.S. is concerned by the joint military drills the People’s Liberation Army is conducting in the Taiwan Strait.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: North Korea is preparing to destroy northern parts of inter-Korean roads, Seoul says
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea said Monday it has detected signs that North Korea is preparing to destroy the northern parts of inter-Korean roads no longer in use, as the rivals are embroiled in soaring tensions over North Korea’s claim that South Korea flew drones over its territory.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel says 4 soldiers killed by Hezbollah drone attack while Israeli strike in Gaza leaves 20 dead
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — A Hezbollah drone attack on an army base in central Israel killed four soldiers and severely wounded seven others Sunday, the military said, in the deadliest strike by the militant group since Israel launched its ground invasion of Lebanon nearly two weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: North Korea sends warning to South Korea, saying troops ready to strike if more drones appear
item.description: North Korea said its army units are prepared to launch strikes against South Korea if another drone carrying propaganda flyers flies into the country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
